title: National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration Sexual Harassment and Assault Prevention Improvements Act of 2025
short_title: National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration Sexual Harassment and Assault Prevention Improvements Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill makes new rules to protect workers at the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration from sexual harassment and assault. It makes it easier and safer for victims to report when bad things happen to them. The bill also requires people who run fishing boats to tell the Coast Guard about any problems. These changes are meant to keep NOAA workers safer while they do their jobs.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Natural Resources.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration Sexual Harassment and Assault Prevention Improvements Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 References
sections.text: Unless this law says otherwise, when it talks about changing or removing parts of another law, it means changing parts of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year two thousand seventeen. This older law deals with ocean and weather agency rules and can be found in section thirty three of the United States Code starting at section eight hundred ninety four.

sections.title: Section 3 Policy on Prevention of and Response to Sexual Harassment Involving National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration Personnel
sections.text: This section changes the existing law about sexual harassment policies. It adds the words and equal employment after sexual harassment everywhere those words appear in the original law. It also adds new reporting requirements. The agency must now include relevant data in their reports. This data includes a short description of each case and what punishment was given if any. It also includes information collected under the Notification and Federal Employee Antidiscrimination and Retaliation Act of two thousand two. This act requires agencies to track discrimination complaints.

sections.title: Section 4 Annual Report on Sexual Harassment Sexual Assault and Equal Employment
sections.text: This section changes the yearly report requirements for the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration. The report title now includes sexual harassment and equal employment along with sexual assault. The report must now cover sexual harassment cases and equal employment issues, not just sexual assaults. For each case involving sexual harassment or equal employment, the report must include a short description and what punishment was given if any. The report must also include several new pieces of information. It must show how many requests were made to move employees to different locations or jobs for safety reasons and how many of these requests were denied. It must show how many cases were reported to the Coast Guard leader. It must include detailed information about cases involving fisheries observers, protected species observers, and endangered species observers. For these observer cases, the report must include a description of each case, the current status, what happened in any investigation, and where the incident happened including what type of fishing area or operation was involved.

sections.title: Section 5 Investigation and Criminal Referral Requirements
sections.text: This section makes several changes to how investigations work. First, it moves some sections of the existing law to different numbers. It changes when cases must be sent to criminal authorities. Instead of only sending cases after an investigation is done, cases can now be sent at the beginning or during an investigation. It also requires that cases involving licensed mariners must be reported to the Coast Guard leader in addition to the United States Attorney. The section adds new rules about when victim information can be shared. Victims can choose restricted or unrestricted reporting. Their personal information can only be shared with administration staff or law enforcement if the victim agrees in writing, if needed to prevent serious danger to the victim or others, if needed to provide victim services, or if required by a court order or law. If information is shared, the agency must try to tell the victim and must protect their privacy and safety. The section requires that within three years, the agency must update its policies to create a better restricted reporting system. This system will let employees, officers, and covered personnel report sexual harassment or assault confidentially. They can get services without their personal information being shared except when necessary for services or as allowed by law. The system will not automatically start an investigation. The section also creates new reporting requirements for vessel operators. The person responsible for a vessel must report any sexual harassment or assault incident to the Coast Guard when it involves administration employees who need merchant mariner credentials or crew members of vessels working under administration contracts. However, incidents reported on a restricted basis do not need to be reported. Reports must be made as soon as the vessel operator learns about the incident using the fastest communication available. Each report must include names, credential numbers, positions, contact information, vessel name and number, time and date, location, and a brief description. The Commerce Secretary must notify the Director of Marine and Aviation Operations about each report received.

sections.title: Section 6 Definitions
sections.text: This section defines important terms used in the law. Administration means the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration. Covered personnel means anyone who works with or does business for the administration. This includes observers, monitors, and other people required to work on commercial fishing vessels or processing facilities to watch for compliance with fishing laws, marine mammal protection, or endangered species protection. It also includes voting members and staff of Regional Fishery Management Councils created under federal fishing laws. Responsible entity of a vessel means the Director of the Office of Marine and Aviation Operations for vessels owned or operated by the administration. Sexual assault has the same meaning as defined in the Violence Against Women Act of nineteen ninety four.

sections.title: Section 7 Conforming and Clerical Amendments
sections.text: This section makes small changes throughout the existing law to keep everything consistent. It replaces the long phrase individuals who work with or conduct business on behalf of the Administration with the shorter term covered personnel everywhere it appears. It also removes the words National Oceanic and Atmospheric from most places in the law except when referring to the full name of this new act, in one specific section heading, and in one definition. The section also updates the table of contents to show the new section numbers and titles that were created by the other changes in this law.

sections.title: Section 8 Prohibited Acts
sections.text: This section changes the federal fishing law called the Magnuson Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act. It removes the word forcibly from the law about interfering with observers. It also removes the phrase on a vessel. These changes make it illegal to interfere with observers in more situations, not just when force is used and not just when it happens on a vessel.

sections.title: Section 9 Prohibition on Service in Commissioned Officer Corps of National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration by Individuals Convicted of Certain Sexual Offenses
sections.text: This section adds a new rule to the law about National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration officers. It references section six hundred fifty seven which deals with prohibiting people convicted of certain sexual offenses from serving as commissioned officers. The section renumbers the existing paragraphs to make room for this new requirement.
